arts.ttl: Paul Merson gives honest prediction of Arsenal's silverware chances this season after being 'destroyed' by Newcastle
arts.sum: Speaking following Arsenal's EFL Cup exit Merson questioned whether Mikel Arteta 's side will be able to add to their trophy cabinet this season.

arts.ttl: 'Oh what a start!' - Birmingham take lead against Newcastle inside a minute
arts.sum: Ethan Laird scores for Birmingham City within a minute to give Birmingham a shock lead over Newcastle in the FA Cup.

arts.ttl: Arsenal 'learn' Alexander Isak asking price as Newcastle 'set valuation' ahead of summer transfer window
arts.sum: Arsenal are allegedly told that Newcastle United will not let star forward Alexander Isak leave St James' Park for anything under £125m.

arts.ttl: Birmingham City v Newcastle United: FA Cup fourth round – live
arts.sum: FA Cup updates, 5.45pm GMT kick-off at St Andrew’s Live scores | Live clockwatch | Leyton Orient 1-2 Man City 6 min Pope, who isn’t the best with his feet, sprays a pass straight out of play. 4 min Iwata’s pass is intercepted by Willock, who surges 50 yards down the left and curls a dangerous cross – or rather, what would have been a dangerous cross if there was a Newcastle player in the area. arts.ttl: How to watch Birmingham City vs Newcastle - TV channel, live steam and FA Cup kick-off time - Birmingham Live
arts.sum: Birmingham City are in FA Cup action on Saturday evening when they take on Premier League side Newcastle United at St Andrew's @ Knighthead Park. Here, BirminghamMail takes a look at the TV info needed to be able to follow Birmingham City's clash with Newcastle in the FA Cup .

arts.ttl: How to watch Birmingham City vs Newcastle United - TV channel, live stream details, kick-off time - Chronicle Live
arts.sum: Newcastle United play League One pace-setters Birmingham City on Saturday with the Magpies looking to avoid an FA Cup fourth round upset. The FA Cup fourth round tie between Birmingham City and Newcastle United will be held on Saturday, February 8.

arts.ttl: May out as Stansfield returns in Birmingham City predicted XI for Newcastle
arts.sum: (4-2-3-1): Allsop; Laird, Klarer, Davies, Cochrane; Iwata, Gardner-Hickman; Dowell, Dykes, Anderson; Stansfield Blues do, of course, have an important League One match against Cambridge United on Tuesday but Newcastle isn’t a game where Davies can afford to hold anything back.
